content.text:
# Code Review — `feat/vault-trust-boundary`

**Scope:** bae5693..HEAD (7 commits, 37 files, 3238 diff lines)
**Intent:** Implement vault trust boundary + lazy retrieval plan — pointer injection, sanctioned retrieval CLI, ask-gated raw/session readers, minimized+rotated logs, deny rules + R2a self-test, docs alignment.
**Mode:** Interactive (pre-PR)
**Run:** `20260421-124829-1227978d` (artifacts in `.context/compound-engineering/ce-review/`)
**Team:** correctness, testing, maintainability, project-standards, agent-native, learnings, security, api-contract, reliability, adversarial, cli-readiness, kieran-typescript (12 reviewers)

## Findings

### P0 — Critical (blocks merge)

| # | File:Line | Issue | Reviewers | Conf | Route |
|---|-----------|-------|-----------|------|-------|
| 1 | src/commands/recall.ts:12-23 | `walkWiki` uses `statSync` (follows symlinks), no realpath containment. A symlink in `wiki/` laundering `sessions/`, `raw/`, or `/etc/passwd` gets emitted as `curation: "curated"`. Directly breaks the trust-boundary's core guarantee. | correctness, security, adversarial | 0.93 | gated_auto |
| 2 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:59-96 | TOCTOU: attacker swaps target to a symlink between `realpathSync` and `readFileSync`. Read-raw excerpt labeled `raw/notes.md` can contain `/etc/passwd`. Trust model assumes vault is adversary-writable. Fix: `open(O_NOFOLLOW)` + `fstat` + `read(fd)`. | correctness, security, adversarial | 0.85 | gated_auto |
| 3 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:93-98 | `--lines=-1` (via `Number()`) bypasses the bounded-excerpt guarantee. `Math.min(-1, 500) = -1`; `slice(0, -1)` returns all-but-last. 500-line raw file yields 499-line "excerpt" with `clamped=false`. Plan promises bounded excerpts. | adversarial, kieran-ts, cli-readiness | 0.95 | gated_auto |

### P1 — High

| # | File:Line | Issue | Reviewers | Conf | Route |
|---|-----------|-------|-----------|------|-------|
| 4 | .claude/settings.json:6-28 | Deny globs hardcoded to `**/cairn/**` and `~/cairn/**`. Users with `CAIRN_VAULT=/opt/notes` have no deny coverage — only TS ask-gate protects. No detection mechanism. | security, adversarial, project-standards, agent-native | 0.88 | manual |
| 5 | .claude/settings.json | Bash deny covers `cat` only on 3 paths. `head`/`tail`/`less`/`grep`/`awk`/`python`/`xxd` all bypass. Absolute paths (no `~`) also bypass. | adversarial, security | 0.90 | manual |
| 6 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:112-128 | `read-raw`/`read-session` — the **most sensitive commands** — emit NO access-log entries. `AccessLogEntry.command` union literally excludes them. Audit trail gap. | security, maintainability, cli-readiness, kieran-ts | 0.90 | gated_auto |
| 7 | src/commands/read-raw.ts:24, read-session.ts:24 | `args.lines ? Number(args.lines) : undefined` — `Number('abc') = NaN`, not nullish. `Math.min(NaN, 500) = NaN`. `slice(0, NaN) = []`. Silent empty envelope, exit 0. | kieran-ts, cli-readiness | 0.91 | gated_auto |
| 8 | src/hooks/inject.ts:52-92 | Hook can emit two JSON envelopes on late failure. `emitContext` fires, then `appendInjectLog` throws, `main().catch` fires `emitEmpty`. Claude Code consumer expects one envelope. | reliability | 0.88 | safe_auto |
| 9 | src/lib/inject/pointer.ts:22-26, src/lib/inject/eager.ts:23-48 | `readFileSync` on index.md/context.md/sessions not wrapped. EACCES/EIO crashes hook silently, agent loses vault pointer with zero signal. | reliability | 0.90 | safe_auto |
| 10 | src/lib/envelope.ts:51-64 | `parseEnvelope` never inspects `schema_version`; casts JSON.parse output to `Envelope` without shape validation. Future v2 silently parsed as v1; malformed payloads crash consumers. | api-contract, kieran-ts, correctness | 0.87 | safe_auto |
| 11 | hooks/inject:6 | Bash shim execs `bun` with no fallback. If bun not on PATH (hermetic invocation, mise not sourced), exec returns 127, SessionStart silently degrades. | reliability, project-standards | 0.85 | gated_auto |
| 12 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:59-68, src/commands/get.ts:38-52 | ScopeDir-is-symlink hijack: if `wiki/`, `raw/`, or `sessions/` directory itself is a symlink, containment checks never verify the scope dir is genuine. | security | 0.75 | gated_auto |
| 13 | src/lib/inject/pointer.ts:22-46 | Lazy-mode pointer payload is free-form prose. `src/hooks/inject.ts:42-49` regexes its own output to count categories. Plan explicitly called out "no ad-hoc delimiter juggling". | agent-native | 0.85 | gated_auto |
| 14 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:109 | `line_range` claims pre-byte-clamp range after bytes truncate mid-file. Envelope lies about which lines are actually present. | correctness | 0.88 | safe_auto |
| 15 | src/lib/inject/modes.ts:28 | Default `inject_mode='eager'` — plan's documented behavior for *existing* installs, but nothing writes `lazy` for fresh installs. Plan Unit 1 explicitly defers this to Unit 5 but Unit 5 didn't implement it either. | api-contract | 0.78 | manual |
| 16 | src/lib/log-writer.ts:13-19 | 2x rotation cycle erases prior archive — audit-trail laundering via log-fill. One archive (`.1`), overwrite on rotate. | adversarial | 0.70 | manual |
| 17 | src/lib/access-log.ts:25-33 | `query_hash` is unkeyed sha256 truncated to 128 bits + exact `query_len` stored. Short queries brute-forceable; length reveals PII. | adversarial | 0.75 | gated_auto |
| 18 | src/commands/recall.ts:71 | Even without the P0 symlink leak, walker labels returned chunks as `wiki/<name>` without realpath-verifying location. | correctness, security | 0.85 | gated_auto |

### P2 — Moderate

| # | File:Line | Issue | Reviewers | Conf | Route |
|---|-----------|-------|-----------|------|-------|
| 19 | src/commands/list-topics.ts:37 | `line_range: [1, lines.length]` lies — returned chunk is heading-only synthesis, not a contiguous slice. | correctness, maintainability, api-contract | 0.92 | safe_auto |
| 20 | src/lib/envelope.ts:12-18 | `EnvelopePolicy` catch-all `[key: string]: unknown` exists solely to smuggle `clamped`. Declare it explicitly; tighten shape. | kieran-ts, project-standards | 0.75 | safe_auto |
| 21 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:110 | Non-exhaustive `scope === "raw" ? ...` mapping. Adding a third scope silently mis-labels. Use exhaustive switch + `never`. | kieran-ts | 0.80 | safe_auto |
| 22 | src/lib/log-writer.ts:13-38 | Rotation failure inside lock aborts append. `renameSync` to pre-existing directory throws; outer `catch {}` swallows whole append. Separate rotation from append. | reliability | 0.82 | safe_auto |

| # | File:Line | Issue | Reviewers | Conf |

| 35 | src/lib/sensitive-read.ts:30 | `isWithin` uses hardcoded `/` separator; Windows mismatch. Use `path.relative`. | correctness | 0.75 |
| 36 | src/commands/recall.ts:27 | `grepFile` silently drops wiki files > 256KB. Surface as `policy.warnings`. | correctness | 0.95 |

| 41 | src/commands/read-raw.ts, read-session.ts | `--approve` description doesn't mention `CAIRN_APPROVE=1` equivalence. Documented only in error message. | cli-readiness, api-contract | 0.70 |

## Coverage

- Suppressed: 0 findings below 0.60 confidence (P0 at 0.50+ retained)
- Untracked files excluded: none
- Failed reviewers: none (all 12 returned)
- **Testing gaps surfaced by reviewers:** symlink-in-wiki test for recall; symlink-swap race for sensitive-read; `CAIRN_VAULT` outside default globs; `--lines=-1/0/NaN/float`; envelope adversarial payloads (embedded sub-envelope, UTF-8 multi-byte); concurrent rotation race; bytes-clamp branch; direct unit tests for `log-writer.ts` and `access-log.ts`; structural (not substring) docs-contract assertions; env pollution in spawn tests (CAIRN_* leakage from host)

## Learnings

- `docs/solutions/` does not exist. No prior institutional learnings corpus to draw from. Worth initializing after this PR lands.

## Requirements Completeness (plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-vault-trust-boundary-lazy-retrieval-plan.md`, source: explicit)

| Req | Status |
|-----|--------|
| R1–R3 trust classification + enforcement | Partially — settings.json deny rules only cover default vault paths (finding #4) |
| R2/R2a release gate + regression detector | Met — hooks/security-self-test |
| R4 sanctioned access API | Met |
| R5 ask-gated reads | Met but audit-gap (finding #6) |
| R5a untrusted→curated crossing w/ provenance | Partially — envelope has provenance but no HMAC binding chunks to nonce (finding #adv-03) |
| R6 lazy pointer payload | Met but prose, not structured (finding #13) |
| R7 pointer <500B | Met |
| R8 length-prefixed envelope | Met but no version-negotiation (finding #10) |
| R10 inject mode config | Met |
| R12 access logs | Partially — sensitive reads missing (finding #6) |
| R13a logs denied via Read/Grep/Glob | Met (in settings.json) |
| R13b minimization + rotation | Met but vulnerable to 2x-rotation laundering (finding #16) |

## Verdict: **Not ready**

Three P0 findings break the trust-boundary's core guarantees:
1. `recall` silently laundering sessions/raw/arbitrary paths as curated (by far the most severe)
2. TOCTOU in sensitive-read (realistic attack given the trust model)
3. `--lines=-1` bounded-excerpt bypass

Fix order:
1. **Before PR (P0s + critical P1s):** #1, #2, #3, #6 (access-log for sensitive reads), #7 (NaN validation), #8 (two-envelope hook bug), #9 (readFileSync try/catch), #10 (envelope schema validation), #11 (bun-missing fallback), #12 (scope-dir hijack)
2. **Strong recommend before PR:** #4, #5 (deny patterns — at minimum, doctor warns when they don't match; Bash deny expanded), #15 (define Unit 5's plan for fresh-install lazy default or explicitly defer), #18 (walkWiki labeling)
3. **Can defer to follow-up:** P2/P3 findings, doc polishing, agent-native enhancements (prose → JSON formats), refactoring

## Policy question

Most P0/P1 findings are `gated_auto` — concrete fixes exist but they change trust-boundary behavior, so I'm not applying them unilaterally. What would you like?

1. **Fix the P0s + critical P1s here, then PR** (Recommended — this code is security-critical)
2. **Review the findings, I fix a subset you approve, then PR**
3. **Push branch as-is, address findings in PR follow-ups**
4. **Stop — further triage needed before fixing**
Fixing P0s + critical P1s in focused commits. Starting with path-safety (P0 #1, #2, #12, and shared helper).

cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/cairn-trust-boundary && git add src/lib/path-safety.ts src/lib/access-log.ts src/lib/sensitive-read.ts src/commands/recall.ts src/commands/get.ts tests/path-safety.test.ts tests/recall.test.ts tests/read-raw.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(security): close recall symlink laundering, harden sensitive-read

Three P0 fixes plus supporting hardening, all centered on a new
src/lib/path-safety.ts helper (isWithin, assertSafeFilename,
assertGenuineScopeDir).

Recall (P0):
- walkWiki now uses lstatSync, skips symlinks, and realpath-checks
  every matched file against realpath(wiki). A symlink inside
  wiki/ can no longer launder sessions/, raw/, or arbitrary
  filesystem content as curated=curated.
- If wiki/ itself is a symlink, the command exits non-zero.

Sensitive read (P0 + P1):
- The approved read path now opens with O_RDONLY | O_NOFOLLOW,
  fstats the fd to confirm regular-file, and reads from the fd.
  Closes the TOCTOU window between realpath and read where an
  attacker could swap the target to a symlink.
- Raw/sessions scope dir is now verified as a real directory
  within the vault (not a symlink) before any file work.
- --lines and --bytes are parsed as strict positive integers;
  invalid values (negative, zero, NaN, float) now exit non-zero
  with a clear "invalid --lines: must be a positive integer"
  message. Previously --lines=-1 yielded a 499-line "excerpt"
  with policy.clamped=false.
- read-raw and read-session now append one entry each to
  access-log.jsonl (command: read-raw|read-session, filename is
  stored as query_hash + query_len, never plaintext). Closes the
  audit-trail gap on the most sensitive commands.
- line_range is recomputed after byte-clamping so the envelope
  stops reporting lines that are not in the returned text.
- assertSafeFilename accepts foo..bar.md but rejects traversal
  segments, absolute paths, backslash traversal, and null bytes.

get (P1):
- Same scope-dir guard as sensitive-read; same shared helpers.

Tests:
- tests/path-safety.test.ts covers the new helper unit by unit.
- tests/recall.test.ts adds three symlink regressions (leak into
  results, leak outside vault, wiki-is-symlink rejection).
- tests/read-raw.test.ts adds scope-dir-is-symlink rejection,
  numeric-bounds rejections (--lines=-1, 0, abc, 1.5; --bytes=-1),
  --bytes clamp + policy.clamped, and access-log hashing.

Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-vault-trust-boundary-lazy-retrieval-plan.md
Review findings: #1, #2, #3, #6, #7, #12, #14, #18, #28, #34

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cairn injection hook — SessionStart and PostCompact
# Delegates to the TypeScript implementation for mode-aware injection
# (eager | lazy | off), pointer payloads, and inject logging.
#
# Fail-soft: if bun is missing or the TS hook errors, emit an empty
# context envelope so SessionStart does not break.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

emit_empty() {
  printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":""}}\n'
}

if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  emit_empty
  exit 0
fi

if ! exec bun "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../src/hooks/inject.ts" "$@"; then
  emit_empty
  exit 0
fi

cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/cairn-trust-boundary && git add src/lib/envelope.ts src/lib/inject/pointer.ts src/lib/inject/eager.ts src/hooks/inject.ts hooks/inject src/commands/list-topics.ts tests/envelope.test.ts tests/list-topics.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(inject,envelope): harden inject resilience + envelope validation

parseEnvelope (P1):
- Validates schema_version === "1" (throws EnvelopeVersionError).
- Validates nonce is 32-hex, policy is an object, chunks is an
  array, each chunk has string source/text, one of the three
  curation values, and a [number, number] line_range.
- Downstream consumers no longer swallow silently-malformed or
  future-version envelopes.
- Adds envelope tests for each rejection path.

Inject hook (P1):
- src/lib/inject/{pointer,eager}.ts now catch readFileSync errors
  (EACCES/EIO/EISDIR) instead of crashing the whole hook. An
  unreadable context.md/index.md/sessions/*.md is treated as
  missing, not fatal.
- src/hooks/inject.ts guards mode resolution and the
  pointer/eager build blocks. An `emitted` flag prevents the
  SessionStart consumer from receiving two JSON envelopes on a
  late-failure path.
- hooks/inject bash shim now probes for bun on PATH before
  exec'ing. If bun is missing, it emits the canonical empty
  envelope and exits 0 instead of returning 127 with no output.

list-topics (P2):
- Each heading is now its own chunk with its actual line_range
  ([n, n]). Previously the single chunk claimed to cover the
  whole file despite only containing heading-synthesis text.

Review findings: #8, #9, #10, #11, #19

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/cairn-trust-boundary && git add .claude/settings.json src/commands/doctor.ts src/lib/vault.ts tests/doctor-trust-boundary.test.ts tests/security-self-test.test.ts tests/init-config.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(security): expand deny patterns, warn on non-default vault path,
default fresh installs to lazy inject mode

.claude/settings.json (P1):
- Bash denies now cover common readers (cat, head, tail, less,
  more, grep, rg, awk, sed, cut, xxd, od, strings, bat, python,
  python3, ruby, node, bun) instead of just `cat`. Patterns use
  `*cairn/raw/*` so absolute paths also match.
- Patterns are still best-effort — the R2a self-test remains the
  regression detector. Shell is not a sandbox.

doctor (P1):
- New "Trust boundary" section warns when the resolved vault path
  does not match either **/cairn/** or ~/cairn/** — those are the
  only path shapes the shipped deny rules cover, so a custom
  CAIRN_VAULT silently escapes host-level enforcement. The CLI
  still protects (ask-gate, envelope, audit log), but the boundary
  becomes CLI-only rather than CLI + host.

init / scaffoldVault (P1):
- Fresh installs now write <vault>/.cairn/config.json with
  {"inject_mode": "lazy"}. Existing vaults with a pre-existing
  state.json are left untouched, preserving eager behavior on
  upgrades. Satisfies the plan's Unit 5 promise of "lazy for
  fresh installs" without changing any resolution precedence.

Tests:
- tests/security-self-test.test.ts asserts Bash denies cover ≥8
  readers and both raw/ + sessions/ scopes.
- tests/doctor-trust-boundary.test.ts exercises the new warning
  for non-default paths and silence for in-scope paths.
- tests/init-config.test.ts asserts fresh install writes lazy
  config.json and existing config.json is preserved.

Review findings: #4, #5, #15

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
# Fixes applied — summary

**Final: 179 pass / 2 fail (pre-existing `entire` detection, unrelated).** Lint clean.

10 commits total (7 implementation + 3 review-fix):

| Commit | Addresses |
|--------|-----------|
| `6e8353d` fix(security): recall symlink laundering + sensitive-read hardening | P0 #1, #2, #3 + P1 #6, #7, #12, #14, #18, #28, #34 |
| `2b0f27e` fix(inject,envelope): resilience + envelope validation | P1 #8, #9, #10, #11 + P2 #19 |
| `09b0631` fix(security): deny expansion + doctor warn + lazy default | P1 #4, #5, #15 |

## What's fixed

**P0 (all 3):**
- Recall no longer follows symlinks in `wiki/` — cannot launder `sessions/`/`raw/`/`/etc/passwd` as curated
- Sensitive-read opens with `O_NOFOLLOW` + `fstat` + read-from-fd — TOCTOU window closed
- `--lines=-1`/`0`/NaN/float now exits non-zero with "invalid --lines: must be a positive integer"

**P1 (10 of 15):**
- Audit log now covers read-raw/read-session (filename hashed, never plaintext)
- Envelope parser validates schema_version, nonce shape, chunks array, curation enum — future `v2` gets `EnvelopeVersionError`
- Inject hook: `emitted` flag prevents two envelopes; readFileSync errors in pointer/eager no longer crash
- Bash shim probes for bun; falls back to empty envelope if missing
- Scope-dir hijack closed: `wiki/`/`raw/`/`sessions/` must be a real directory inside vault
- `.claude/settings.json` now denies ~19 Bash reader tools (was just `cat`)
- `doctor` warns when resolved vault path escapes default deny globs
- `scaffoldVault` writes `{inject_mode: "lazy"}` on fresh installs
- `list-topics` emits one chunk per heading with real line numbers

## Deferred to follow-up (noted but not fixed here)

- **P1 #13** pointer payload prose → structured envelope (agent-native improvement, not security)
- **P1 #16** rotation cascade (keep N archives; requires retention policy decision)
- **P1 #17** HMAC for query_hash (needs per-vault key management)
- **All P2/P3** (duplicated helpers, readline SIGINT handling, doctor `--format=json`, agent-native enhancements, lockfile stale tuning, etc.)

## Ready for PR

Branch state: 10 commits on top of `feat/session-capture-manifest` HEAD (implicit dependency on session-manifest landing first).

Push + PR options:
1. **Push + open PR now** (stacked on session-manifest — merge order: session-manifest first, then this)
2. **Push branch only** (no PR yet; wait for session-manifest landing)
3. **Hold** (keep local, address more follow-up items first)

Pick one.

input.command:
cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/cairn-trust-boundary && gh pr create --base feat/session-capture-manifest --head feat/vault-trust-boundary --title "feat: vault trust boundary + lazy retrieval (Layer 1+2)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Ships Layer 1 + Layer 2 of Cairn's trusted/lazy/observable memory story — a sanctioned CLI gate between agents and the vault so untrusted surfaces (`raw/`, `sessions/`, `.cairn/*.jsonl`) stop appearing in agent context.

- **Inject modes** — `hooks/inject` is now a TS hook with `lazy | eager | off` modes. Lazy mode emits a <500B pointer payload (topic categories + recall hint) instead of dumping sessions.
- **Sanctioned retrieval CLI** — `cairn recall`, `cairn get`, `cairn list-topics` wrap output in a length-prefixed JSON envelope (`{schema_version, nonce, policy, chunks: [{source, line_range, curation, text}]}`) with validated schema.
- **Ask-gated reads** — `cairn read-raw <filename>` and `cairn read-session <filename>` provide bounded excerpts of untrusted surfaces. Fail closed in non-interactive contexts unless `--approve` or `CAIRN_APPROVE=1`. Uses `O_NOFOLLOW` + `fstat` + read-from-fd to close the TOCTOU window between realpath and open.
- **Observability** — `.cairn/inject-log.jsonl` + `.cairn/access-log.jsonl`. Entries carry hashed filenames/queries only, never plaintext. Rotates at 2 MB under `withExclusiveLock`.
- **Host-level deny rules** — ships plugin-root `.claude/settings.json` denying Read/Grep/Glob on raw/sessions/logs and ~19 common Bash readers (cat, head, tail, less, grep, rg, awk, sed, xxd, od, strings, bat, python, python3, ruby, node, bun, …).
- **R2a regression detector** — `hooks/security-self-test` emits deterministic probes + sentinel files an agent can run to confirm denies haven't drifted. `cairn doctor` now also warns when the resolved vault path escapes the default deny globs.
- **Docs/skills alignment** — `templates/CAIRN.md` and `skills/{cairn,extract,refine}/SKILL.md` teach agents the new surfaces table, ask-gate workflow, and untrusted-excerpt-is-data framing.

## Stack

Base: `feat/session-capture-manifest` (foundation libs `src/lib/{excerpt,manifest,lockfile}.ts`). Land that PR first, then this one. On clean main this PR is 11 commits; most are cleanly separable units.

## Implementation Units (all checked)

- [x] Unit 1: inject modes + pointer payload (`1b15231`)
- [x] Unit 2: recall/get/list-topics + envelope (`32dc137`)
- [x] Unit 3: read-raw/read-session + ask-gate + bounds (`5d20d7d`)
- [x] Unit 4: access/inject log w/ minimization + rotation (`2d28e37`)
- [x] Unit 5: `.claude/settings.json` + R2a self-test (`6828620`)
- [x] Unit 6: docs/skills alignment (`a64ee78`)

## Review findings addressed (pre-PR)

Ran `/ce:review` with 12 reviewers. 3 P0s + 10 of 15 P1s fixed in three follow-up commits before opening this PR:

**`6e8353d` fix(security): recall symlink laundering + sensitive-read hardening**
- P0: `recall` no longer follows symlinks in `wiki/`; cannot launder sessions/raw/outside-vault content as curated.
- P0: `read-raw`/`read-session` now open with `O_RDONLY | O_NOFOLLOW` + `fstat` + read-from-fd. Closes the TOCTOU race.
- P0: `--lines=-1` / `0` / `NaN` / `1.5` / `abc` now exit non-zero with `invalid --lines: must be a positive integer` (previously yielded a 499-line "excerpt" with `clamped=false`).
- P1: `read-raw`/`read-session` now emit access-log entries (filename hashed, never plaintext).
- P1: scope-dir hijack — `wiki/`/`raw/`/`sessions/` must be a real directory inside the vault.
- Extracted shared `src/lib/path-safety.ts` (`isWithin`, `assertSafeFilename`, `assertGenuineScopeDir`).

**`2b0f27e` fix(inject,envelope): resilience + envelope validation**
- P1: `parseEnvelope` validates `schema_version === "1"`, nonce shape, chunks array, curation enum. Future v2 throws `EnvelopeVersionError`.
- P1: inject hook can no longer emit two JSON envelopes on a late failure; `readFileSync` errors in pointer/eager builders no longer crash the hook.
- P1: `hooks/inject` bash shim probes for `bun`; falls back to the empty envelope if missing instead of returning exit 127.
- P2: `list-topics` now emits one chunk per heading with accurate `line_range`.

**`09b0631` fix(security): deny expansion + doctor warn + lazy default**
- P1: `.claude/settings.json` Bash denies now cover common readers (cat, head, tail, less, more, grep, rg, awk, sed, cut, xxd, od, strings, bat, python, python3, ruby, node, bun). Patterns use `*cairn/raw/*` so absolute paths also match.
- P1: `cairn doctor` warns when the resolved vault path doesn't match `**/cairn/**` or `~/cairn/**` — the only path shapes the shipped denies cover.
- P1: `scaffoldVault` writes `<vault>/.cairn/config.json` with `{"inject_mode": "lazy"}` on fresh installs. Existing installs untouched.

## Deferred to follow-up (not blocking this PR)

- Pointer payload: prose → structured envelope (agent-native improvement)
- Rotation: keep N archives with retention policy (currently 1 archive)
- HMAC for query_hash (requires per-vault key mgmt)
- Unify exit codes across retrieval commands (currently all 1)
- Readline SIGINT / stdin-close handling in ask-gate prompt
- Agent-native JSON output formats (`cairn doctor --format=json`, security-self-test `--format=json`)
- Structural (non-substring) docs-contract test assertions
- Concurrent-rotation stress test

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test` — 179 pass / 2 pre-existing fails (entire CLI detection, unrelated to this change)
- [x] `bunx tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [ ] Manual smoke: fresh install → `cairn init` writes `inject_mode: lazy`; SessionStart pointer payload <500B; `cairn recall auth` returns curated chunks only
- [ ] Manual smoke: `cairn read-raw foo.md` without approval exits non-zero; with `--approve` returns bounded excerpt
- [ ] Manual smoke: `bash hooks/security-self-test` generates probes; agent session attempts probes and confirms denies fire

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10
